comment_text.sn22.151:1.2: Kiṁ upādāya might be rendered as “derived from what”, but the fact that it is parallel with abhinivissa (“insist”) shows that the sense “grasping” is prominent. Grasping is the “taking up” of the view of self, which hardens into “insistence”.
comment_text.sn22.151:1.5: This is another context that emphasizes the “existence” of the aggregates (see SN 22.94:1.5 and note).

root_text.sn22.151:1.2: “Kismiṁ nu kho, bhikkhave, sati, kiṁ upādāya, kiṁ abhinivissa:
root_text.sn22.151:1.3: ‘etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā’ti samanupassatī”ti?
root_text.sn22.151:1.5: “rūpe kho, bhikkhave, sati, rūpaṁ upādāya, rūpaṁ abhinivissa …pe…
root_text.sn22.151:1.6: viññāṇe sati, viññāṇaṁ upādāya, viññāṇaṁ abhinivissa:
root_text.sn22.151:1.7: ‘etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā’ti samanupassati.
root_text.sn22.151:1.11: vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, api nu taṁ anupādāya etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attāti samanupasseyyāti?
root_text.sn22.151:1.18: vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, api nu taṁ anupādāya etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attāti samanupasseyyāti?

translation_text.sn22.151:1.2: “Mendicants, when what exists, because of grasping what and insisting on what,
translation_text.sn22.151:1.3: does someone regard things like this: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn22.151:1.4: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. …”
translation_text.sn22.151:1.5: “When form exists, because of grasping form and insisting on form …
translation_text.sn22.151:1.6: When consciousness exists, because of grasping consciousness and insisting on consciousness,
translation_text.sn22.151:1.7: someone regards it like this: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self.’
translation_text.sn22.151:1.11: “But by not grasping what’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, would you regard it like this: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn22.151:1.18: “But by not grasping what’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, would you regard it like this: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn22.151:1.21: They understand: ‘… there is nothing further for this place.’”
